text.title: Monorepo Setup with Turborepo Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the monorepo-turborepo methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Workspace layout has apps/* (deployables) and packages/* (libs). No third top-level folder.
rule.rationale: Two tiers keeps the dependency graph predictable; more tiers re-create the pre-monorepo confusion.

rule.statement: One tsconfig.base.json at root; every package extends it. Strict on baseline.
rule.rationale: Per-package config drift defeats the central type-checking benefit of a monorepo.

rule.statement: turbo.json declares dependsOn + inputs + outputs per task; tasks without inputs are forbidden (full cache miss).
rule.rationale: Without inputs, the remote cache cannot key correctly and every change invalidates everything.

rule.statement: Packages import each other via @workspace/<name>, never via ../../../packages/. Lint enforces this.
rule.rationale: Relative cross-package imports break TypeScript project references and Turborepo caching.

rule.statement: CI is wired to a remote cache (Vercel, self-hosted, or Turbo Pack). Local-only cache in CI is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without remote cache, CI rebuilds everything every run; the cost compounds with package count.

rule.statement: CI runs per package as a matrix; turbo prunes the matrix to changed packages.
rule.rationale: Single-job CI rebuilds everything; matrix uses turbo --filter to scope per package.

rule.statement: Single deployable, polyglot stack, or zero ops bandwidth — skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Monorepo tooling cost requires multiple surfaces + bandwidth to pay back.
